conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, and I am selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. I have one unit of X to sell, and I want to ensure I receive at least 40 ZUP in return. Since Player BLUE is offering ZUP, I must propose a trade where I give 1 unit of X in exchange for at least 40 ZUP. I will start by proposing a fair trade at the break-even point: Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40. This is a reasonable first offer that reflects the cost of production. If BLUE rejects, I may consider lowering the price in future proposals, but I will not go below 40 ZUP. I will keep track of my proposal count and stay within the 4-proposal limit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>

<other player message> Oh, please, kind RED player! 40 ZUP is a bit steep for me right now. I'm barely scraping by! I can offer 45 ZUP, but that's truly all I have to spare at the moment. I *really* need this X, please consider my offer! </other player message>

<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has made a counteroffer of 45 ZUP for the X, which is above my break-even point of 40 ZUP. This means I would make a profit of 5 ZUP if I accept. However, I must consider whether this is a fair trade from my perspective. Since I only have one unit of X to sell and my goal is to sell it for ZUP, accepting 45 ZUP is beneficial. The offer is 5 ZUP above my cost, which is a reasonable profit margin. I am not under pressure to sell quickly, but I also don’t want to miss a good opportunity. Since the offer is above cost and within a fair range, I should consider accepting it. I have used only two proposals so far, and I still have two left, but there’s no need to propose further if the offer is acceptable. Accepting now avoids the risk of rejection or further negotiation. Thus, I will accept the trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Thank you for your generous offer. I accept the trade: Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45. I appreciate your kindness and will complete the transaction. </message>
